items.title: Chinese-launched rocket lights up Australian skies as it heads for orbit
items.summary: Some skywatchers describe a silvery or blue-tinted ball of gas that gradually turned red and disappeared.

items.title: Mould, overcrowding and housing delays affecting families in Wilcannia
items.summary: Delays in maintenance repairs, including mould removal, are forcing families to sleep on mattresses in living rooms, adding to overcrowding pressures in remote homes.

items.title: Serena Williams makes winning return to court at Queen's in doubles match
items.summary: Serena Williams steals the show at Queen's Club, as the American icon marks her eagerly awaited return to competitive action with a doubles victory nearly four years after her last match.

items.title: Australia records first ODI loss to Bangladesh in 21 years as hosts win by 86 runs
items.summary: Bangladesh posts just its second victory over Australia in 23 ODIs, recording an 86-run win in the opening match of the series in Dhaka.

items.title: Call for security boost at shopping centres to clamp down on knife risk
items.summary: A retail worker at Sydney's Broadway Shopping Centre says she was left scared after a man told her he had a knife.

items.title: Man found dead in a park in Donvale had suffered burns, police say
items.summary: Police say the man appeared to have suffered burns and was discovered by a passer-by in parkland in Melbourne's east.

items.title: Hitchhiker tells NSW missing persons inquiry of Ivan Milat encounter
items.summary: Etched in Steven Clark's memory are the protruding teeth and moustache of the driver he says picked him up as a teenager — a man he now believes was serial killer Ivan Milat.

items.title: Xi Jinping's North Korea visit is unusual, and shows his need to court Kim Jong Un
items.summary: Xi Jinping and Kim Jong Un's bilateral talks in North Korea were a big departure from the Chinese president's usual diplomacy playbook because, for this first time this year, he was the one travelling abroad to make the meeting happen.

items.title: Gary Ablett Jr, John Worsfold among Australian Football Hall of Fame inductees
items.summary: Six new members are inducted into the Australian Football Hall of Fame, including Geelong and Gold Coast legend Gary Ablett Jr.

items.title: Bus overcrowding prompts push for more Brisbane transit lanes
items.summary: Buses are Queensland's most popular form of public transport, but advocates say a lack of investment means they are often unreliable and overcrowded.

items.title: Palestinian father vows to fight for justice for seven-month-old baby killed by Israeli troops
items.summary: A Palestinian father promises to fight for justice for his seven-month-old son, who was shot dead by an Israeli soldier in the West Bank city of Hebron.

items.title: New 50 cent airport bus could cost disheartened Townsville business
items.summary: An airport shuttle taking passengers through the heart of a regional city will start competing with public transport later this month.

items.title: National security concerns over use of transcription company VIQ Solutions by 13 government agencies
items.summary: Thirteen government agencies are now embroiled in an expanding scandal involving potential data breaches prompting calls for an urgent audit amid concerns over national security.

items.title: Melbourne councils say state government task force to crack down on illegal dumping is ineffective
items.summary: Councils across Melbourne's suburbs are struggling to combat the rise of illegal dumping, despite over $21 million of funding from the Victorian state government.

items.title: Some countries are protecting a child's right to play — not Australia
items.summary: The first time Renae Powell waved her eldest child through the primary school gates she felt something was missing: play time.

items.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: When does it start, who are the favourites, what time do the Socceroos play?
items.summary: The FIFA World Cup is the biggest single-sport event on the planet and has delivered numerous historical moments, from Diego Maradona's "hand of God" in 1986 to Zinedine Zidane's infamous headbutt on Marco Materazzi.

items.title: Victim-survivors tear up agreement with Catholic church, claiming hypocrisy
items.summary: Victim-survivors of clergy abuse say negotiations and agreements reached with the Catholic Diocese of Ballarat are now null and void.

items.title: ABC is looking for storytellers for International Day of People with Disability
items.summary: If you are an emerging content maker or journalist with disability and have a story to tell, now is your chance to work with the ABC to tell it.

items.title: Fuel shock spurs business uptake of green equipment including batteries and EVs
items.summary: The Iran war and the oil supply squeeze are supercharging business investment in renewables.

items.title: How Rolf Harris hid in plain sight
items.summary: Rolf Harris's conviction for child sexual abuse was shocking to those who saw him as a lovable larrikin. But the people who were abused by him are refusing to stay silent about the impacts of his crimes.

items.title: Travel quiz, June 3: Stow your luggage in the locker overhead, we're off on a trip
items.summary: The lek is the currency of which country? And what's the Japanese word for pufferfish, the sashimi delicacy that's toxic if prepared incorrectly?

items.title: Trump vows to respond after Iran shoots down helicopter patrolling Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: US President Donald Trump accuses Iran of shooting down a US Apache helicopter that was patrolling the Strait of Hormuz, vowing to retaliate.

items.title: Northern Ireland police appeal for calm after asylum seeker arrested over Belfast stabbing
items.summary: Protesters hold anti-immigration demonstrations in Northern Ireland and England after a Sudanese man is arrested over a "brutal" knife attack in Belfast.

items.title: Tens of thousands displaced after magnitude-7.8 earthquake in Philippines
items.summary: Rescuers are combing through collapsed buildings in the southern Philippines after a powerful earthquake devastated coastal communities.

items.title: Teens recruited by crime syndicates for arson attacks
items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: Whittlesea mayor Aidan McLindon's suspension invalid, Supreme Court rules
items.summary: The Supreme Court finds the state government had no basis on which to recommend Aidan McLindon suspension from his role as the mayor of Whittlesea.

items.title: Encrypted apps including Signal and Discord used by criminals to lure teens with intellectual disabilities into crime
items.summary: Criminal syndicates are targeting vulnerable children with no criminal history, low IQ and intellectual disabilities to carry out their dirty work through encrypted apps.

items.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
items.summary: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

items.title: Priscilla Hon calls out prize money disparity at Libéma Open tennis tournament
items.summary: Priscilla Hon draws attention to the difference between the men's and women's winners' prize money at the Libéma Open in The Netherlands.

items.title: Confidence assured in Macquarie Point Stadium delivery at meeting of AFL presidents
items.summary: Despite suggestions that potential delays and cost blowouts of Tasmania's new stadium may be on the minds of AFL heads, the topic was barely mentioned at the presidents' annual meeting.

items.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Disney on Ice spoiled the sport's finals plans, so how does it secure better venues?
items.summary: The 2025 Super Netball Grand Final set a new standard with a record crowd at one of the biggest stadiums in the country. But in 2026, the sport has been forced to retreat to smaller finals venues due to a scheduling issue with Disney on Ice.

items.title: Mongols bikie Brandt Graham flees the Darwin Local Court

items.title: Iran says ticket allocation for World Cup withdrawn days before tournament
items.summary: Iran's football federation says the 8 per cent quota of tickets allocated to Iran for the FIFA World Cup has been revoked, meaning "there is no possibility to distribute tickets to fans".

items.title: Man jailed for running over and seriously injuring SA Police officer
items.summary: A man, who was on the run from authorities in Victoria, has been jailed for eight years after mowing down a police officer in South Australia.

items.title: University of Notre Dame stops taking new nursing students for another year
items.summary: The university says it will not take new students for its nursing program for another year, as it needs to "rebalance enrolments across the program within accredited program capacity".

items.title: Year 12 students at Melbourne's Mazenod College found cheating using AI
items.summary: An all-boys school in Melbourne's outer east has taken action after 35 students were caught cheating on a Year 12 exam using AI.

items.title: Di Blessing praises 'hero' husband's bravery during alleged Torrington home invasion
items.summary: Stabbing victim Di Blessing praises her husband's bravery during an alleged home invasion in northern New South Wales.

items.title: Police officers 'acted appropriately' during Clare arrest death incident, SA's top cop says
items.summary: SA Police Commissioner Grant Stevens says a preliminary view of the body worn camera vision from an incident at Clare on the weekend where a man died during an arrest shows officers "acted appropriately".

items.title: Iron ore miner Nathan River Resources enters administration owing at least $300 million
items.summary: Iron ore mining company Nathan River Resources has gone into voluntary administration owing at least $300 million in debt, unpaid wages and royalties.

items.title: Cape York figures charged over alleged misuse of Indigenous corporation funds
items.summary: Dion Creek, Naomi Hobson and Tim Jaffer are all facing multiple charges relating to the alleged misuse of money from two Aboriginal corporations.

items.title: Acting SA electoral commissioner calls for council elections to be delayed
items.summary: South Australia's acting electoral commissioner says she wants council elections delayed until mid-2027 while it awaits a review into the drama-plagued state and Voice elections earlier in the year.

items.title: Aboriginal woman pepper-sprayed by guards during self-harm incident at Canberra's jail
items.summary: The use of pepper spray on an Aboriginal woman who was self-harming after being denied access to a cultural arts program was "inappropriate and should not happen," according to the ACT's prison watchdog.

items.title: Barbeques Galore to close more than 60 stores, with hundreds of workers facing redundancy
items.summary: It will shut its 62 company-owned stores in the coming weeks and working through "transitional arrangements" with 27 stores owned by franchises, with hundreds of staff facing redundancy.

items.title: Police upgrade assault charges to murder after death of Darwin man Martin Wiberg
items.summary: Police have charged three men who allegedly beat 48-year-old Darwin man Martin Wiberg to death in a "vicious attack" at the Casuarina bus stop earlier this year with his murder.

items.title: NDIS inquiry warned changes could push families into crisis and towards child protection services
items.summary: Exhausted families could be plunged further into crisis and have greater interaction with child protection services under the proposed overhaul of the NDIS, advocates say.

items.title: Former WA Police officer's insurance claim over horrific service injury denied
items.summary: Former WA Police officer and WA Australian of the Year Paul Litherland says he is "heartbroken" after his claim for post-service medical compensation over injuries he received in the force was denied.

items.title: Fugitive Mongols bikie Brandt Graham arrested in Palmerston after five-day manhunt
items.summary: Police have arrested fugitive Top End Mongols bikie Brandt Graham five days after the 44-year-old escaped from custody last week, sparking an "extensive manhunt".

items.title: Crew rescued after US helicopter goes down near Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Donald Trump confirms reports of a US Apache helicopter crash near the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: City of Hobart e-bike library program a success and helping to drive sales
items.summary: A free e-bike library is set to continue in Hobart after a successful first year and a reported rise in sales, as more people make the switch to e-bikes.

items.title: Medical imaging provider to investigate improper Medicare billing allegations
items.summary: One of Australia's largest medical imaging providers, Integral Diagnostics, says it will look into allegations of improper Medicare billing at a regional WA public hospital where the company provides radiology services.

items.title: Police say 17yo fired 30 shots at wrong funeral venue for Sydney crime boss
items.summary: Two people are charged over a shooting at what was advertised as the venue for a Sydney crime boss's funeral.

items.title: Brody Mihocek has surgery on neck fracture sustained in Demons' eight-point AFL win over Magpies
items.summary: Brody Mihocek is recovering from surgery to fix a neck fracture after he was stretchered off the MCG during the AFL King's Birthday match.

items.title: Sitaya Fagan set to make Opals debut against China ahead of FIBA Women's Basketball World Cup
items.summary: Teenage sensation Sitaya Fagan is given her first Opals call-up as coach Sandy Brondello turns her focus towards the FIBA Women's Basketball World Cup.

items.title: US President Donald Trump booed at NBA Finals clash after havoc outside Madison Square Garden
items.summary: US President Donald Trump cops the vitriol of the Madison Square Garden crowd after causing chaos to attend the NBA Finals.

items.title: Idris Elba shuts down idea of a black James Bond
items.summary: Idris Elba has quashed rumours he was ever in the running to play James Bond, saying audiences are unlikely to come along for the ride if Bond is played by a black actor.

items.title: Nauiyu community leaders say residents will return to flood ravaged homes with no services
items.summary: Three months ago the town of Nauiyu / Daly River was submerged in the region's worst ever floods. Now, Indigenous leaders worry residents are returning to a community with no services, if the NT and federal government do not step in.

items.title: Severe storm leaves homes flooded in South West WA town Harvey
items.summary: An unexpected deluge has flooded homes and public facilities in the South West WA town of Harvey. The community was pelted with 40mm of rain in one hour.

items.title: Why I waited until almost 30 to take my first trip overseas
items.summary: As I approached the age of 30, I longed to travel outside of Australia, but there was always an excuse or obstacle. A chance to share my culture with the world finally got me there.

items.title: Doctors say more packaged foods should have health star ratings
items.summary: Health bodies want star ratings on all packaged foods to help shoppers make healthier choices, but the grocery industry says the voluntary system works.

items.title: New leader, old problem for WA Liberals as Basil Zempilas flirts with One Nation
items.summary: As Basil Zempilas flirts with One Nation, will the right-wing populist party drag the WA Liberals down again, or will it become a life raft on the way to a political resurrection?

items.title: Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan vows to 'see it through' despite party discontent
items.summary: The Victorian premier said she had no plans to step aside despite some Labor MPs becoming increasingly frustrated by her performance as leader and more worrying opinion polls.

items.title: Blues drop Haumole Olakau'atu for State of Origin II, Dylan Lucas to debut against Maroons
items.summary: Blues coach Laurie Daley confirms his starting line-up ahead of next week's State of Origin II against Queensland at the MCG.
